message:
fix(analytics): address edge cases and deprecation warnings

Fix several edge cases and one deprecation warning in analytics code.

apps/web/lib/analytics.ts:

1. Fix deprecated String.substr() usage:
   - Changed: .substr(2, 9) → .slice(2, 11)
   - substr() is deprecated in favor of slice()
   - slice(2, 11) produces same 9-character substring

2. Fix potential crash in trackOutboundLink():
   - Added try/catch around new URL(url) parsing
   - Invalid URLs (e.g., malformed user input) no longer throw
   - Falls back to "unknown" for link_domain on parse failure

3. Fix division by zero in trackLandingCTA():
   - scrollableHeight can be 0 when page is not scrollable
   - Added check: scrollableHeight > 0 ? calculation : 0
   - Wrapped in IIFE for clean inline expression
   - Prevents NaN/Infinity values in scroll depth tracking

apps/web/lib/hooks/useWizardAnalytics.ts:

4. Fix uninitialized ref timing issue:
   - Changed: useRef<number>(0) → useRef<number>(Date.now())
   - Ensures startTime has valid timestamp immediately
   - Prevents 0 or undefined values in time calculations
   - More accurate step duration tracking from first render

These are defensive programming improvements that prevent runtime
errors and analytics data quality issues in edge cases.
